steps.title: Look for an increase
steps.content: The researcher claims that some lakes have seen an increase in ice duration even though the general trend is decline. Supporting data must show a lake with fewer ice days at the start of the study and more ice days at the end.

steps.title: Reject nonmatching data
steps.content: Choice B compares Kitusjarvi and Mirror Lake by latitude and typical ice duration, but it does not show change over time. Choice C says Kitusjarvi had more ice days in 1980-81 than in 2005-06, which is a decrease. Choice D also compares two lakes rather than tracking one lake's change.

steps.title: Choose the time trend
steps.content: The remaining choice says Lake Baikal had fewer ice days in 1980-81 than in 2005-06. That describes an increase in duration across the study period, directly supporting the researcher's claim that some lakes increased. The answer is A.
